identity.title: Prove the equivalent-work cross-boundary vertical slice

authorization.reference: Operator authorized Phase 0 planning, requested WP-FF-008 through WP-FF-011 as detailed no-context STUB packets linked to the canonical design and readiness topics, and required the stubs to be committed and pushed before WP-FF-003 implementation begins.

blockers: This is the design Section 28 Phase 2 proof slice; it cannot start merely because the STUB is indexed.
WP-FF-003 through WP-FF-010 applicable Phase 0 gates and evidence must be accepted, including compatibility, contracts, transport, archive, resource/durability, and FFmpeg supervision.
The Section 28 Phase 1 coarse workspace/lifecycle skeleton and independent watcher must be VALIDATED by a future taskboard packet before this slice starts.
Broad feature implementation remains blocked until all Section 31.7 readiness gates that this slice consumes are proven.
lifecycle.next_action: After Phase 0 readiness gates and the Phase 1 skeleton are mechanically proven, bind this packet to exact prerequisite closure SHAs and corpus/tool manifests, refresh benchmark research, promote it, and implement the complete Section 28 Phase 2 slice without dropping any boundary or comparing unequal work.

scope.summary: Integrate one representative end-to-end path across direct HTTP and HLS acquisition, serializable selection, atomic resources/byte credits, FFmpeg stream copy, durable staged commit/archive, independent watcher diagnostics, and an equivalent-work comparison against the pinned yt-dlp oracle.
in_scope: Acquire a pinned direct-HTTP fixture and representative HLS fixture through the accepted transport and protocol contracts.
Carry a serializable source graph, stable identities, format selection, logical ordering, and typed errors/events through the same engine path.
Use atomic resource-vector admission and end-to-end byte credits with observable bounds, cancellation, and no hidden unbounded stage.
Run FFmpeg stream-copy discovery, explicit stream maps, progress, cancellation, descendant containment, reaping, and ffprobe-normalized validation.
Perform same-filesystem staged commit, resume, injected-crash reconciliation, archive insertion, and cleanup without reporting success before output/archive reconciliation.
Emit independent watcher crash evidence and alive-but-stalled detection while proving watcher absence/failure cannot change product correctness or result.

Compare Ferric against pinned yt-dlp 2026.07.04 only after normalized source, requests, selected streams, output/ffprobe, metadata/sidecar/archive work, failure class, cache state, and process topology are equal.
Use randomized paired order, warmup, repetitions, outlier/noise policy, distributions, and 95-percent confidence bounds; report total-system and separated Ferric/FFmpeg costs.
Run cancellation, retry, slow-disk, crash-prefix, watcher-failure, and output-mismatch negative cases with machine-readable reports.
Add the thin YouTube/challenge canary only if Phase 0C selected an authorized passing challenge and transport path; otherwise record it as blocked without substituting a runtime.
non_goals: Full extractor, protocol, collection, live, sink, post-processing, or yt-dlp replacement parity.
A speed claim based on different work, output, metadata, archive action, validation, cache, network state, or process topology.
Using live internet measurements as the primary gate.
Adding Python, production yt-dlp, an external JavaScript runtime, SQLite native bindings, or another unapproved dependency.
Making the watcher a controller, acknowledgement dependency, or product success dependency.
Treating one mean, one machine, or one successful happy-path run as proof.
dependencies: WP-FF-003 through WP-FF-010 applicable closure commits validated in main
A future Phase 1 coarse workspace/lifecycle/watcher packet validated in main
Pinned yt-dlp 2026.07.04 oracle artifact and mandatory fixture/corpus hashes
Approved transport, archive, challenge-if-used, resource/durability, and FFmpeg implementations
Versioned benchmark manifest/report schema and reproducible Windows/Unix test profiles
FF-GATE-ARCH-001, FF-GATE-PR-001, FF-GATE-DEEP-001, and applicable watcher gate operational

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Pinned direct-HTTP and HLS fixtures complete through one engine path with serializable source/selection identities, typed events/errors, and normalized output facts matching the accepted oracle predicates.

Resource and byte-credit reports prove atomic admission, declared item/byte bounds, cancellation progress, no permit/credit leaks, and no hidden unbounded buffering under normal, slow-consumer, retry, and cancellation cases.
FFmpeg stream-copy uses a trusted pinned identity, explicit maps and -c copy, bounded progress/pipes, full descendant cancellation/reaping, and ffprobe validation before commit eligibility.

Resume and fault tests cover every durable commit/archive prefix and prove idempotent reconciliation, no resume beyond durable_contiguous, no corrupt final output, and no success before output/archive agreement.

Watcher tests record Ferric crash evidence and alive-but-stalled state while missing, crashed, incompatible, hung, or storage-failed watcher cases leave identical product behavior and decided result within bounded startup/shutdown.

Every admitted benchmark sample passes equality for source graph, request sequence, bytes, selected streams, ffprobe output, metadata/sidecar/archive action, validation, failure class, cache/network state, and declared process topology; deliberately cheaper work is rejected.

The versioned benchmark report records randomized paired order, warmup, repetitions, outlier/noise policy, distributions, 95-percent confidence bounds, total-system CPU/wall/RSS/I-O, and separated Ferric/FFmpeg costs; one mean cannot pass.
FF-GATE-ARCH-001, FF-GATE-PR-001, triggered deep/model/fault/benchmark gates, FF-GATE-WATCHER-001, and independent adversarial review pass against one source/tool/corpus/benchmark manifest.

checks_run: git status --porcelain=v2 --branch: main at base before packet creation
git worktree list --porcelain: one main worktree; no packet worktree created
blockers: Packet is a STUB and Phase 2 prerequisites are not yet proven.
last_checkpoint.next_action: Keep indexed as STUB until Phase 0 gates and a validated Phase 1 skeleton exist.

promotion_requirements: Bind every prerequisite packet to a VALIDATED closure commit in main.
Confirm all Section 31.7 gates consumed by the slice are executable and passing.
Validate the future Phase 1 skeleton and independent watcher before Phase 2 work.
Freeze exact fixture, oracle, toolchain, FFmpeg, archive, transport, host, filesystem, process-topology, cache, and benchmark manifests.
Record current source-level benchmark and cross-boundary integration research plus rejected alternatives.
Freeze equality predicates before performance thresholds.

documentation_topics.heading: Behavioral parity, measurement, and bounded concurrency
Benchmark Matrix
documentation_topics.use: B-005 through B-010, B-016, B-018 through B-021

readiness_matrix_items.required_resolution: Equivalent work first; versioned workloads, distributions, and confidence bounds second.

readiness_matrix_items.gate: Equivalent-work benchmark manifests with correctness gates and confidence intervals

readiness_matrix_items.output: Direct-HTTP plus HLS plus FFmpeg-stream-copy vertical slice with cancel, resume, crash reconciliation, and equivalent-work benchmark
readiness_matrix_items.note: v0.3.0 places the integrated slice in Phase 2 after Phase 0 proof gates and the Phase 1 skeleton; the later canonical design sequencing controls activation.

high_roi_additions.addition: Use one provenance-bound run manifest as the join key for oracle equality, product diagnostics, fault traces, watcher evidence, and benchmark samples.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: Every surface already needs provenance, and one key prevents mismatched evidence and parallel-agent confusion.
high_roi_additions.gap_closed: Reports from different source/tool/corpus states could otherwise be combined falsely.
high_roi_additions.validation: Aggregator rejects any report with a different manifest hash.

high_roi_additions.addition: Retain deterministic replay bundles for every failed cross-boundary run.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: The fixtures and traces already exist, so packaging them makes future regression and parallel model work much cheaper.
high_roi_additions.gap_closed: Network/process/crash failures are otherwise difficult to reproduce.
high_roi_additions.validation: A fresh no-context run reproduces the normalized verdict offline.

high_roi_additions.addition: Add equality-rejection fixtures that intentionally omit archive work, choose a lower-quality stream, warm only one cache, or shift topology.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: The benchmark gate is already being built and these cheap negatives prevent the most likely false speed claims.
high_roi_additions.gap_closed: A polished benchmark could reward cheaper work.
high_roi_additions.validation: Each unequal fixture is rejected before timing statistics are evaluated.

gaps_closed: No watcher proof on real slice

red_team.risk: Ferric appears faster by doing less work.
red_team.verification: Every deliberate shortcut is rejected before statistics.

red_team.risk: Happy path hides boundedness or cancellation failure.
red_team.verification: Saturation and cancellation cases meet declared bounds and leave no corrupt output/orphan.

red_team.risk: Crash reconciliation produces false success or inconsistent archive.
red_team.verification: Fault every prefix and rerun recovery twice with identical final verdict.

red_team.risk: Benchmark result is statistically or environmentally invalid.
red_team.verification: Report validator rejects missing/mismatched fields and unstable noise above threshold.

red_team.risk: The STUB is activated before Phase 2 prerequisites.
red_team.verification: Promotion check fails unless every named prerequisite is VALIDATED in main.

performance_contract.claim_rule: Upper confidence bound must satisfy no-regression; lower bound must satisfy any claimed improvement; a single mean never passes.

verification_needs.closure_rule: No performance or slice PASS unless all correctness/resource/recovery/watcher/equality gates pass on the exact same provenance manifest and every prerequisite closure is contained in main.
